fieldDescription: Set this to true to disable all features this mod provides, effectively turning this into a library mod.

This must be enabled to use the more cheat like commands this mod provides.
If Dev mode is on, this will be treated as enabled, even if it isn’t.

Adds a bunch of useful hotkeys in many places.

Hotkeys:

CTRL Click
Fleet member: Copies clicked member
Officer portrait: Copies clicked officer

CTRL + C in
Fleet screen: Copies own fleet
Interaction screen: Copies fleet encounter (hold alt to avoid copying supporting fleets)
Refit screen: Copies variant
Codex: Copies variant/member

Hold shift when copying if you want to copy a user viewable and editable version in JSON instead of the default behavior of copying a compressed string.

CTRL + V in
Refit Screen: Add variant in clipboard to the corresponding ship hull’s autofit menu.

CTRL + I in
Campaign: Open Save Transfer dialog

CTRL + SHIFT + D
Anywhere: Open developer options. You can toggle dev mode here.

THE BELOW REQUIRES CHEATS TO BE ENABLED

CTRL + V in
Fleet screen: Add the copied fleet/member/variant/officer to your fleet
Campaign: Spawn fleet in the clipboard into the campaign

CTRL + O in
Campaign: Open officer creator dialog

When true, automatically randomizes the ship name and officer name/portrait if present when pasting an element with them.

This avoids pasted members looking like a clone army, and makes them look like a bunch of ships/officers that happen to have the same loadout instead
A clickable filter on the fleet screen that allows you to type in keywords to filter visible ships.

defaultValue:
You can enter hull names like "Hammerhead", or ship types such as "phase", "carrier", or "frigate".

Ships that do not match any of the entered keywords will be hidden.
To exclude specific matches, prepend a dash (-) to your entry; for example, -frigate will hide all frigates.
Filters are separated by spaces. Surround your filter with quotes “ “ to be more exact.

See the entire list of inputs by hovering over the filter to see the tooltip.
fieldDescription: Fleet Screen Filter Field
Functions similar to the Fleet Filter, but for the hull mod picker.

Enable the setting ‘Show Hidden HullMods’ in the ‘Autofit Menu’ tab and hover over a ship in the autofit menu to see its hidden hullmods, provided it has them.

Enabling this causes an autofit to remove all contents of your current variant and replace it with the selected one. At no cost.
This is default behavior in the main menu missions, as there is no cargo or story points in that context.
If Overwrite Variant on Autofit is enabled, don’t remove D-Mods on an autofit.
If Overwrite Variant on Autofit is enabled, don’t remove S-Mods on an autofit.

Officers stay with their ships when putting a ship in storage, thus removing them from your fleet.
Upon retrieving the ship from storage, the officer returns to your fleet as its pilot.

Remove your player officer with a right click: (places them in a command shuttle that can't fight or be copied) (Commander bonus still applies)
This is enabled by default if Cheats are enabled.
When recovering a new ship: mothball it.
Turn the transponder off when entering hyperspace

Shows the ship preview earlier during derelict salvage.

Instead of requiring “Consider Special Measures” to view the ship, the preview is available a step earlier when you get the option to salvage it.

Right click a storage submarket to pull up the Cargo Auto Manager.

This menu allows you to configure automatically taking and/or depositing cargo items on entering or leaving a market.
You can, for example, automatically stock up on supplies, fuel, and crew on interaction with a market.
You can also automatically deposit all weapons when interacting with a market
Highly configurable
If true, will show a message at the top of the screen every time the Cargo Auto Manager moves your cargo on entering or leaving a market.

fieldName: Fix Missing Source Mods on Ship Skins
fieldDescription:
Fixes various issues where a source mod would be expected but not present.
By default, ship skins (e.g. Sunder XIV from ship weapon pack) do not have a source mod assigned; this option applies a source mod to each hull skin where relevant.
As this is considered fixing a base game issue, it is enabled by default.
Changes to this setting will only take effect after game restart.

Examples:
Ensures ship skins correctly display their originating mod in the codex.
Ensures ships save/copy in this mod with their sourceMod noted down, allowing the user to know where a ship originated if missing the mod that added it.
And more.

When cheats are enabled, a button is added to the Codex that lets you add the currently viewed entry to your fleet.
Hold Shift or Alt to multiply the added value by 10
Hold CTRL to add a blueprint of the codex entry to your fleet’s cargo instead of the entry itself. Provided the entry has a blueprint.

If true, right click a hullmod in the refit screen to remove it. This works on SMods, built in mods, DMods, etc.
Cheats must be enabled.

After every save, a save transfer copy of the save is made.
This might be useful if your save game gets bricked and you can’t load it.
Put “loadsave -backup” in the console to load it.

If true, prevents error messages from appearing at the top of the screen.
Error messages will still appear in the console and logs.

I couldn’t get this one to work properly, but I left it in case anyone would like to try it out anyway. It technically works somewhat.

When this mod creates a variant, such as a Lion’s Guard variant with a default DMod, should it be removed by default?

When an autofit variant is being created from file after being saved, it creates a new “HULL” variant with nothing on it to place the contents of the variant on it.
The “HULL” variant of certain ships, such as Lion Guard ships, by default come with a DMod.
This causes newly loaded autofit variants to come with DMods despite the data for what the autofit variant should have on it not having it.

This being true simply makes the behavior expected, only have a DMod if the saved variant has one.

Relates to autofit.
Only save SModdedBuiltIns if the variant has the corresponding built-in hullmod.
This prevents hullmods such as the Mad Rockerpiper MIDAS from Roider Union from being saved, as that hullmod is an sModdedBuiltIn without the ship having the MIDAS hullmod in the hullspec of the ship

fieldName: Clean game variants for removed elements
fieldDescription:
On application load, look through every variant loaded within the entire game and remove any elements which cannot be found.
Includes removed weapons, wings, and hull-mods.
This will, for example, prevent saved mission variants from causing the mission to fail to load.
It may also prevent game crashes in rare examples.
Show debug elements

Enable debug elements. While not limited to this, it causes some redundant code to run to check my results.
Essentially test cases.
Compared to showDebug, nothing should appear to the user (aside from possible lag) if everything is working correctly.
